examples.text:
عُذْرُ الْمَلُومَيْنِ فِيكَ أَنَّهُمَا
 آسٍ جَبَانٌ وَمِبْضَعٌ بَطَلُ

senses.gloss: to be valiant, to be heroic
to be or become null, void, futile, worthless, pointless

examples.english: When the reason is known, the wonder is lost.

senses.gloss: to nullify, to invalidate, to render ineffective, to rescind, to repeal, to revoke

etymology.text:
From Proto-Semitic *baṭal-.

The verb form I is inherited from Proto-Semitic *baṭal-.

See the etymology of the corresponding lemma form.

body.text: , said to be the only epithet of its measure except
strong-hearted, on the occasion of war,
valour, a brave man, a hero;
whose wound goes for nothing, so that he does not care for it,
and it does not withhold him from the exercise of his courage;
the blood of whose adversaries goes for nothing with him,

(TA:) or for this reason he is thus called; (TA;) or because life is annulled, or made to go for nothing, on the occasion of encountering him, and severe misfortunes are annulled by him, (Msb,) or by his sword, and made to be of no account: (TA:) and so ↓
applied to a woman; (S, Msb, K;) accord. to one of the expositors of the Hamáseh; (Msb;) but AZ says that this is not allowable: (IDrd, TA:) the pl. of
